slides.notes: Keep clean with minimal text. Include all author affiliations.
Frame the research question clearly. Why does this matter?

slides.content_focus: Literature context, prior work, theoretical framework
slides.notes: Brief and focused. 3-5 key references maximum.
Numbered list of 2-3 objectives. Clear and testable.

slides.content_focus: Study design, population, interventions, analysis plan
slides.notes: Visual study design diagram preferred. Include sample size.

slides.content_focus: Primary outcome, main finding, key figure or table
slides.notes: One key finding per slide. Use figures over tables when possible.
Include only if time permits. Highlight key comparisons.
Optional. Use for robustness checks or supplementary findings.

slides.content_focus: Interpretation, comparison to literature, clinical implications
slides.notes: Compare findings to 2-3 key prior studies. State implications.
Acknowledge 3-4 key limitations. Show awareness without undermining.

slides.content_focus: Key takeaways, 3-4 bullet points, future directions
slides.notes: 3-4 concise takeaway messages. End with next steps or future work.
Include funding source logos if available. Add contact information.
